results.possibleIdentity: Robert Weinberg was a physicist and educator active in the late 1960s and 1970s, known for his involvement in physics education and collaborations between science and the arts. He served as a professor of physics at Stony Brook University until approximately 1973, when he moved to Temple University in Philadelphia. During his tenure at Temple, he was an advocate for innovative teaching methods and was noted for his sympathy toward the student body. Weinberg was associated with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, where his technical expertise was cataloged for potential collaborations with artists. His technical fields of interest included mathematics, computers, programming, and various areas of physics such as optics, spectroscopy, color theory, and light. He also worked with specialized radiations including X-rays, ultraviolet light, cathode rays, and the field of cybernetics. Notably, he was a supporter of the Exploratorium in San Francisco, writing in support of its investment in science education and experimental pedagogy.
